content_html:
The runtime governance layer — and the SP/1.0 conformance standard — for autonomous AI agents. SHACKLE mediates every agent tool call in real time: runaway token loops, unhandled tool cascades, and budget overruns are stopped before the next call fires. It is live, it runs today, and its reference implementation provably passes its own conformance suite. This is not a proposal or a prototype — it is a working standard you can run, verify, and be certified against. (Cost is read from usage after each call, so SHACKLE halts the loop, not the single call that crossed the line; see the AGPL best-effort disclaimer below.)

🔒 SP/1.0 — The Conformance Standard

SHACKLE is not only a runtime circuit breaker — it is the authored, verifiable conformance standard for runtime mediation of agent tool calls.

  • Decision surface: ALLOW / DENY / HITL
  • Conformance model: Valid(τ) ⇔ Required(τ) ⊆ Supported(τ)
  • 15 hash-verifiable conformance vectors in fixtures/conformance.json — 10 decision-core + 5 HITL transition cases (approve / reject / modify / defer-escalate / duplicate-resume)
  • Pure reference implementation: shackle/conformance.py — a stdlib-only decide(config, state, call) -> (verdict, reason)
  • Executable proof: pytest tests/test_conformance.py runs every vector against the reference
  • Core invariant: history-visible ≠ runtime-executable — a record that an action happened is not proof the transition was supported

A runtime is SHACKLE-conformant iff it passes the published fixture set — provable by reproduction, not assertion. See CONFORMANCE.md for the full specification and how to claim conformance. The fixture hashes have been independently reproduced by third parties.

Which layer is which: shackle/conformance.py + fixtures/conformance.json are the conformance-verified layer — the authored spec and its 15 hash-verifiable vectors, with a reference decide(). shackle/core.py is the shipped runtime integration (the @Guard decorator): it maps its live TriggerEngine/ExecutionState onto decide()'s (config, state, call) contract and consults the same reference decide() on every evaluated tool call and every LLM call, recording the verdict on state.last_decision. So "SP/1.0-conformant" refers to the spec, the fixtures, and the reference implementation the shipped runtime actually calls — one decision surface, not two implementations that happen to agree.

Authorship & provenance: SHACKLE, the Required ⊆ Supported conformance model, the decide() surface, and the HITL transition contract are authored by Dante Bullock (@Fame510), sole author. First published 2026-06-17.

📜 What SP/1.0 Actually Is

SP/1.0 (SHACKLE Protocol 1.0) is a conformance standard for runtime mediation of autonomous AI agent actions. In plain terms: it defines — precisely, and in a way anyone can independently verify — what it means for a system to correctly decide whether an agent is allowed to take its next action.

Every AI agent, at every step, is about to do something: call a tool, spend budget, invoke another agent, execute a transaction. SP/1.0 governs that moment. It specifies three things no other agent framework pins down as a verifiable contract:

  1. The decision surface — ALLOW / DENY / HITL. Every mediated action resolves to exactly one verdict: allow it, deny it, or halt for a human. There is no fourth state and no ambiguity. Each verdict carries a deterministic, inspectable reason.
  2. The conformance model — Valid(τ) ⇔ Required(τ) ⊆ Supported(τ). A transition is valid if and only if everything it requires is within what the system provably supports. This is the mathematical spine of the standard: capability is a set relationship, not a promise.
  3. The core invariant — history-visible ≠ runtime-executable. The fact that an action is recorded, resumed, or replayed is not evidence that it was ever authorized. A rejected or deferred action that comes back around is denied, not waved through. This single rule closes the class of failures where agents "resume" their way past their own guardrails.

SP/1.0 ships as 15 hash-verifiable conformance vectors (fixtures/conformance.json) — 10 decision-core cases plus 5 human-in-the-loop transition cases (approve / reject / modify / defer-escalate / duplicate-resume) — and a stdlib-only reference implementation (shackle/conformance.py). You do not take the standard on faith. You run it. pytest tests/test_conformance.py executes every vector against the reference, and the reference implementation passes its own suite — verified, not asserted.

🎓 What It Means to Be SHACKLE Certified

SHACKLE Certification is proof — not a promise — that a runtime correctly enforces the SP/1.0 mediation contract. It is measured entirely against the public, hash-verifiable fixtures. The same vectors that certify you are the ones anyone else can re-run to check your claim. There is no private audit, no trust-us seal, no pay-to-pass: certification is reproduction.

For a buyer, "SHACKLE Certified" answers the question every serious acquirer, enterprise, and regulator eventually asks about an autonomous system: "Can you prove it does what it's allowed to do — and nothing else?" A certified runtime can. In minutes. From a clean clone.

Certification Levels

Level Name What it guarantees
SP/1.0-Core Core Conformance The runtime resolves every mediated action to the correct ALLOW / DENY / HITL verdict, with the correct deny reason. The decision surface is sound.
SP/1.0-HITL Transition-Complete Core, plus correct handling of every human-in-the-loop transition: approve, reject, modify, defer-escalate, and duplicate-resume. Rejected and deferred actions provably cannot execute by being replayed.
SP/1.0-Sovereign Enterprise Runtime HITL, plus atomic daemon state, tamper-evident ledgering, and audit export. The full accountability layer an enterprise or acquirer can hold to account.

How to Get Certified (open to any runtime, today)

  1. Run the suite against your runtime: pytest tests/test_conformance.py and pytest v2/tests/test_v2_conformance.py.
  2. Capture the evidence. SHACKLE emits a per-fixture pass/fail report with the fixture hashes it verified against. That report is your conformance artifact — independently reproducible by anyone.
  3. Submit for listing. Open a certification request with your report and a public, reproducible evidence link. An automated check re-runs the public fixtures; a maintainer verifies before listing.
  4. Get listed. Verified runtimes appear in the public SHACKLE Conformance Registry with the level achieved, SP/1.0 version, and date — and may display the SP/1.0 Certified badge.

An open standard, an open door

SP/1.0 is a neutral, public standard. Any agent runtime — including competing frameworks and competing safety products — is invited to test against it and be listed. The fixtures are public. The verdicts are deterministic. The registry is open. We hold our own reference implementation to exactly the bar we ask of everyone else, and we publish our fixtures so the claim is checkable.

That is the entire philosophy: a standard is only worth what it can withstand. If a runtime conforms, the registry proves it. If it doesn't, the fixtures show exactly where. Either way, the measure is public and the same for everyone.

Certify your agents. → Run the fixtures · Get listed · View the registry

Integrations (LiteLLM + AutoGen)

SHACKLE now ships first-class governance for the biggest chokepoints in the agent stack. See INTEGRATIONS.md for full usage and LiteLLM proxy config.yaml examples.

  • LiteLLM guardrail (shackle/litellm_shackle_guardrail.py) — enforce SP/1.0 on any LiteLLM-powered agent (CrewAI, AutoGen, LangGraph, custom):
    • ShackleGuardrail — Option A, backed directly by the pure SP/1.0 reference decide() (conformance-exact, dependency-light).
    • ShackleEngineGuardrail — Option B, driven by the full TriggerEngine (stateful budget / repeat / timeout).
    • Both provide sync check()/record() for the SDK and async_pre_call_hook/async_post_call_success_hook for the LiteLLM proxy. litellm is an optional dependency.
  • AutoGen wrapper (shackle/autogen_shackle_wrapper.py) — wrap_tool decorator + create_shackle_agent factory that govern AutoGen tool calls through the same engine. AutoGen is optional; wrap_tool works without it.

One integration = coverage for the whole supply chain: because LiteLLM sits under most agent frameworks, the guardrail governs them through a single chokepoint. All decisions trace to the hash-pinned fixtures in fixtures/conformance.json.

Provenance

SHACKLE was built by Dante Bullock, a 52-year-old self-taught systems architect and engineer out of Oakland, California. No venture capital. No corporate incubator. Just raw necessity and a refusal to watch autonomous agents burn money in silent infinite loops.

Rather than guessing what the agent ecosystem needed, Sovereign Logic used real-time web scraping and community sentiment mining to audit the issue trackers of CrewAI, AutoGen, and LangGraph — mapping the exact systemic failures affecting developers in production, then building the drop-in circuit breaker to fix them.

This is infrastructure built by a developer, for developers — sovereign, lean, and zero-bloat.

🎯 When to Use SHACKLE

SHACKLE is purpose-built for:

  • Local development and debugging — Interactive HITL console gives you real-time control
  • CLI agents and supervised workflows — Resume/Skip/Abort when loops are detected
  • Cross-framework coverage — One decorator works across CrewAI, LangGraph, and AutoGen
  • Budget enforcement — Client-side token tracking prevents runaway costs
  • Iterative testing — Catch loops early in the development cycle

For headless production APIs (serverless functions, FastAPI endpoints, background workers where blocking for human input isn't an option), consider framework-native solutions like TokenCircuit for automated LangGraph overrides.

SHACKLE and production-oriented tools solve complementary problems: use SHACKLE during development and testing, then transition to automated overrides for deployed APIs if needed.

⚡ The Problem

AI agents are highly capable, but their error-handling is fundamentally broken. When an agent hits an unhandled tool error (401 Unauthorized, changed API payload, dead endpoint), it rarely self-corrects. Instead, it enters a "Loop of Death" — retrying the exact same tool with the exact same input, burning your context window and running up massive API bills in minutes.

Frameworks like CrewAI, AutoGen, and LangGraph lack native, framework-agnostic spending guardrails or deterministic loop breakers.

🛡️ The Solution

SHACKLE is a lightweight, zero-dependency governance layer that sits inside your runtime via dynamic Python shims. It intercepts LLM calls and tool executions client-side, monitoring execution state deterministically.

When an agent breaches your boundaries, SHACKLE trips the circuit breaker, halts execution, and drops you into an interactive terminal console.

Key Features

  • 1-Line Install — no refactoring your agent topology
  • Loop of Death Prevention — detects identical sequential tool calls and error cascades
  • Budget Enforcement — real-time token tracking against a client-side pricing table
  • Execution Timeouts — prevents hung threads on dead APIs
  • HITL Console — interactive terminal with Resume / Skip / Abort options
  • 100% Client-Side — no telemetry, no phone-home, no hidden SaaS

🚀 Quick Start

1. Install

Note: Shackle is published on PyPI as the pyshackle package (the import name stays shackle).

# From source (available now)
git clone https://github.com/Fame510/SHACKLE.git
cd SHACKLE
pip install -e .

# From PyPI:
pip install pyshackle

2. Guard Your Workflow

from shackle import Guard
from crewai import Crew, Agent, Task

# Your normal CrewAI setup
my_crew = Crew(agents=[...], tasks=[...])

# One line to add circuit breaking
@Guard(budget=0.25, max_repeat_calls=3, timeout_seconds=180)
def run():
    return my_crew.kickoff()

run()

That's it. SHACKLE dynamically hooks the underlying interpreters — no CrewAI source changes needed.

⚙️ The Four Circuit Breakers

Trigger Condition Default What Happens
REPETITIVE_TOOL_CALL Same tool + same input called N times, or input contains error signals 3 attempts Drops to HITL console
BUDGET_EXCEEDED Accumulated token cost exceeds limit (via local pricing table) $0.20 Hard execution freeze
TIMEOUT_REACHED Wall-clock execution exceeds threshold 180 seconds Immediate halt
MAX_TOOL_CALLS Total tool invocations exceed limit 50 calls Hard stop

Error Loop Amplification

SHACKLE amplifies sensitivity when tool inputs contain error signals (401, 500, timeout, unauthorized, etc.) — catching the "I'll just try again" loop before the agent burns tokens on a permission error it can't fix.

🛠️ The HITL Console

When a breaker trips, SHACKLE renders an interactive terminal:

⛓️ SHACKLE CIRCUIT BREAKER: REPETITIVE_TOOL_CALL

Agent:         ResearchAgent
Tool:          web_search
Input:         {"query": "latest AI news", "error": "401 Unauthorized"}
Call Count:    3x
━━━ Session Stats ━━━
Tokens:        In: 8,400 | Out: 1,200
Session Cost:  $0.02850
Time Running:  47.2s

Options:
  [R] Resume/Reset — clear history, continue execution
  [S] Skip — return dummy output, attempt context flush
  [A] Abort — hard terminate the current run

Select action (R/S/A):

🔌 Works With

Framework Support Notes
CrewAI ✅ Full litellm hook + BaseTool hook + Agent.execute_task (experimental)
LangChain / LangGraph Sync + async litellm (completion/acompletion) + BaseTool (run/arun) hooks cover sync and async paths
AutoGen ✅ Full litellm interception catches all LLM calls
Smolagents 🧪 Experimental Manager Agent reasoning loop detection active

🚀 V2: Enterprise Runtime Sovereignty Layer (Optional)

For production deployments requiring distributed state, compliance audit logs, or remote agent control, see v2/README.md.

V2 adds:

  • ✅ Distributed budget tracking (across serverless functions, Lambda, K8s)
  • ✅ Postgres audit logs (cryptographically signed, SOC2-ready)
  • ✅ Remote HITL control (manage headless agents from mobile/web)
  • ✅ Commercial licensing (for closed-source products)

V1 (this) is always free and perfect for local development. V2 is an optional upgrade for enterprise production use.

SHACKLE is open-source under AGPLv3 — free for individual developers, hobbyists, and open-source projects. If you're using SHACKLE in a closed-source commercial product, SaaS platform, or enterprise deployment, the AGPLv3 requires you to open-source your entire application. Most companies don't want to do that — so they purchase a commercial license instead.

🤝 Contributing

Pricing Table Updates

As model providers update pricing, submit PRs to shackle/core.py → MODEL_PRICING. Contributors who submit verified pricing updates get credited in release notes.

Adding Framework Hooks

SHACKLE's architecture supports pluggable runtime hooks. To add support for a new framework:

  1. Add a _patch_<framework>() function following the pattern in core.py
  2. Register it in _apply_patches()
  3. Submit a PR with integration tests

💼 Commercial Support (optional)

SHACKLE is free and open source (AGPLv3). If you want hands-on help deploying it in your stack, paid implementation and architecture-audit support is available.

SHACKLE dynamically hooks the underlying interpreters — no CrewAI source changes needed. ⚙️ The Four Circuit Breakers Trigger Condition Default What Happens REPETITIVE_TOOL_CALL Same tool + same input called N times, or input contains error signals 3 attempts Drops to HITL console BUDGET_EXCEEDED Accumulated token cost exceeds limit (via local pricing table) $0.20 Hard execution freeze TIMEOUT_REACHED Wall-clock execution exceeds threshold 180 seconds Immediate halt MAX_TOOL_CALLS Total tool invocations exceed limit 50 calls Hard stop Error Loop Amplification SHACKLE amplifies sensitivity when tool inputs contain error signals (401, 500, timeout, unauthorized, etc.) — catching the "I'll just try again" loop before the agent burns tokens on a permission error it can't fix. 🛠️ The HITL Console When a breaker trips, SHACKLE renders an interactive terminal: ⛓️ SHACKLE CIRCUIT BREAKER: REPETITIVE_TOOL_CALL Agent: ResearchAgent Tool: web_search Input: {"query": "latest AI news", "error": "401 Unauthorized"} Call Count: 3x ━━━ Session Stats ━━━ Tokens: In: 8,400 | Out: 1,200 Session Cost: $0.02850 Time Running: 47.2s Options: [R] Resume/Reset — clear history, continue execution [S] Skip — return dummy output, attempt context flush [A] Abort — hard terminate the current run Select action (R/S/A): 🔌 Works With Framework Support Notes CrewAI ✅ Full litellm hook + BaseTool hook + Agent.execute_task (experimental) LangChain / LangGraph Sync + async litellm (completion/acompletion) + BaseTool (run/arun) hooks cover sync and async paths AutoGen ✅ Full litellm interception catches all LLM calls Smolagents 🧪 Experimental Manager Agent reasoning loop detection active 🚀 V2: Enterprise Runtime Sovereignty Layer (Optional) For production deployments requiring distributed state, compliance audit logs, or remote agent control, see v2/README.md. blocks.html_en: The runtime governance layer — and the SP/1.0 conformance standard — for autonomous AI agents. SHACKLE mediates every agent tool call in real time: runaway token loops, unhandled tool cascades, and budget overruns are stopped before the next call fires. It is live, it runs today, and its reference implementation provably passes its own conformance suite. This is not a proposal or a prototype — it is a working standard you can run, verify, and be certified against. (Cost is read from usage after each call, so SHACKLE halts the loop, not the single call that crossed the line; see the AGPL best-effort disclaimer below.)

blocks.html_en: Which layer is which: shackle/conformance.py + fixtures/conformance.json are the conformance-verified layer — the authored spec and its 15 hash-verifiable vectors, with a reference decide(). shackle/core.py is the shipped runtime integration (the @Guard decorator): it maps its live TriggerEngine/ExecutionState onto decide()'s (config, state, call) contract and consults the same reference decide() on every evaluated tool call and every LLM call, recording the verdict on state.last_decision. So "SP/1.0-conformant" refers to the spec, the fixtures, and the reference implementation the shipped runtime actually calls — one decision surface, not two implementations that happen to agree.

blocks.html_en: 📜 What SP/1.0 Actually Is

sentences.en: 📜 What SP/1.0 Actually Is
sentences.zh: 📜 SP/1.0 到底是什么

blocks.html_en: SP/1.0 (SHACKLE Protocol 1.0) is a conformance standard for runtime mediation of autonomous AI agent actions. In plain terms: it defines — precisely, and in a way anyone can independently verify — what it means for a system to correctly decide whether an agent is allowed to take its next action.

sentences.en: SP/1.0 (SHACKLE Protocol 1.0) is a conformance standard for runtime mediation of autonomous AI agent actions.
sentences.zh: SP/1.0（SHACKLE 协议 1.0）是自主 AI 代理操作运行时调解的一致性标准。

sentences.en: In plain terms: it defines — precisely, and in a way anyone can independently verify — what it means for a system to correctly decide whether an agent is allowed to take its next action.
sentences.zh: 简而言之：它精确地并且以任何人都可以独立验证的方式定义了系统正确决定是否允许代理采取下一步行动的含义。

blocks.html_en: Every AI agent, at every step, is about to do something: call a tool, spend budget, invoke another agent, execute a transaction. SP/1.0 governs that moment. It specifies three things no other agent framework pins down as a verifiable contract:

sentences.en: Every AI agent, at every step, is about to do something: call a tool, spend budget, invoke another agent, execute a transaction.
sentences.zh: 每个人工智能代理的每一步都要做一些事情：调用工具、花费预算、调用另一个代理、执行交易。

sentences.en: SP/1.0 governs that moment.
sentences.zh: SP/1.0 支配着那个时刻。

sentences.en: It specifies three things no other agent framework pins down as a verifiable contract:
sentences.zh: 它指定了其他代理框架没有规定为可验证合约的三件事：

blocks.html_en: • The decision surface — ALLOW / DENY / HITL. Every mediated action resolves to exactly one verdict: allow it, deny it, or halt for a human. There is no fourth state and no ambiguity. Each verdict carries a deterministic, inspectable reason.

sentences.en: The decision surface — ALLOW / DENY / HITL.
sentences.zh: 决策面 — 允许/拒绝/HITL。

sentences.en: Every mediated action resolves to exactly one verdict: allow it, deny it, or halt for a human.
sentences.zh: 每一项调解行动都会做出一个判决：允许、拒绝或停止。

sentences.en: There is no fourth state and no ambiguity.
sentences.zh: 没有第四种状态，也没有歧义。

sentences.en: Each verdict carries a deterministic, inspectable reason.
sentences.zh: 每个判决都有一个确定性的、可检验的理由。

blocks.html_en: • The conformance model — Valid(τ) ⇔ Required(τ) ⊆ Supported(τ). A transition is valid if and only if everything it requires is within what the system provably supports. This is the mathematical spine of the standard: capability is a set relationship, not a promise.

sentences.en: The conformance model — Valid(τ) ⇔ Required(τ) ⊆ Supported(τ).
sentences.zh: 一致性模型 — 有效 (τ) ⇔ 必需 (τ) ⊆ 支持 (τ)。

sentences.en: A transition is valid if and only if everything it requires is within what the system provably supports.
sentences.zh: 当且仅当转换所需的一切都在系统可证明支持的范围内时，转换才是有效的。

sentences.en: This is the mathematical spine of the standard: capability is a set relationship, not a promise.
sentences.zh: 这是该标准的数学支柱：能力是一组关系，而不是承诺。

blocks.html_en: • The core invariant — history-visible ≠ runtime-executable. The fact that an action is recorded, resumed, or replayed is not evidence that it was ever authorized. A rejected or deferred action that comes back around is denied, not waved through. This single rule closes the class of failures where agents "resume" their way past their own guardrails.

sentences.en: The core invariant — history-visible ≠ runtime-executable.
sentences.zh: 核心不变量——历史可见≠运行时可执行。

sentences.en: The fact that an action is recorded, resumed, or replayed is not evidence that it was ever authorized.
sentences.zh: 记录、恢复或重放某个操作的事实并不能证明该操作曾被授权。

sentences.en: A rejected or deferred action that comes back around is denied, not waved through.
sentences.zh: 被拒绝或推迟的行动被拒绝，而不是被拒绝。

sentences.en: This single rule closes the class of failures where agents "resume" their way past their own guardrails.
sentences.zh: 这条规则结束了代理“恢复”越过自己护栏的故障类别。

blocks.html_en: SP/1.0 ships as 15 hash-verifiable conformance vectors (fixtures/conformance.json) — 10 decision-core cases plus 5 human-in-the-loop transition cases (approve / reject / modify / defer-escalate / duplicate-resume) — and a stdlib-only reference implementation (shackle/conformance.py). You do not take the standard on faith. You run it. pytest tests/test_conformance.py executes every vector against the reference, and the reference implementation passes its own suite — verified, not asserted.

sentences.en: SP/1.0 ships as 15 hash-verifiable conformance vectors (fixtures/conformance.json) — 10 decision-core cases plus 5 human-in-the-loop transition cases (approve / reject / modify / defer-escalate / duplicate-resume) — and a stdlib-only reference implementation (shackle/conformance.py).
sentences.zh: SP/1.0 提供 15 个可哈希验证的一致性向量 (fixtures/conformance.json) — 10 个决策核心案例加上 5 个人机循环转换案例（批准/拒绝/修改/延迟升级/重复恢复） — 以及仅 stdlib 的参考实现 (shackle/conformance.py)。

sentences.zh: 你没有凭信心接受这个标准。

sentences.en: You run it.
sentences.zh: 你运行它。

blocks.html_en: 🎓 What It Means to Be SHACKLE Certified

sentences.en: 🎓 What It Means to Be SHACKLE Certified
sentences.zh: 🎓 获得 SHACKLE 认证意味着什么

blocks.html_en: SHACKLE Certification is proof — not a promise — that a runtime correctly enforces the SP/1.0 mediation contract. It is measured entirely against the public, hash-verifiable fixtures. The same vectors that certify you are the ones anyone else can re-run to check your claim. There is no private audit, no trust-us seal, no pay-to-pass: certification is reproduction.

sentences.en: SHACKLE Certification is proof — not a promise — that a runtime correctly enforces the SP/1.0 mediation contract.
sentences.zh: SHACKLE 认证是运行时正确执行 SP/1.0 中介契约的证明（而不是承诺）。

sentences.en: It is measured entirely against the public, hash-verifiable fixtures.
sentences.zh: 它完全是根据公共的、可哈希验证的装置来衡量的。

sentences.en: The same vectors that certify you are the ones anyone else can re-run to check your claim.

blocks.html_en: • Run the suite against your runtime: pytest tests/test_conformance.py and pytest v2/tests/test_v2_conformance.py.

blocks.html_en: An open standard, an open door

sentences.en: An open standard, an open door

blocks.html_en: SP/1.0 is a neutral, public standard. Any agent runtime — including competing frameworks and competing safety products — is invited to test against it and be listed. The fixtures are public. The verdicts are deterministic. The registry is open. We hold our own reference implementation to exactly the bar we ask of everyone else, and we publish our fixtures so the claim is checkable.

sentences.en: SP/1.0 is a neutral, public standard.
sentences.zh: SP/1.0 是一个中立的公共标准。

sentences.en: Any agent runtime — including competing frameworks and competing safety products — is invited to test against it and be listed.
sentences.zh: 邀请任何代理运行时（包括竞争框架和竞争安全产品）对其进行测试并列出。

sentences.en: The fixtures are public.
sentences.zh: 赛程是公开的。

sentences.en: The verdicts are deterministic.
sentences.zh: 判决是确定性的。

sentences.en: The registry is open.
sentences.zh: 注册表已打开。

sentences.en: We hold our own reference implementation to exactly the bar we ask of everyone else, and we publish our fixtures so the claim is checkable.
sentences.zh: 我们将自己的参考实现与我们向其他人提出的要求完全一致，并且我们发布了我们的固定装置，以便可以检查声明。

blocks.html_en: That is the entire philosophy: a standard is only worth what it can withstand. If a runtime conforms, the registry proves it. If it doesn't, the fixtures show exactly where. Either way, the measure is public and the same for everyone.

sentences.en: That is the entire philosophy: a standard is only worth what it can withstand.
sentences.zh: 这就是整个哲学：标准只有它能够承受的范围才有价值。

sentences.en: If a runtime conforms, the registry proves it.
sentences.zh: 如果运行时符合要求，注册表就会证明这一点。

sentences.en: If it doesn't, the fixtures show exactly where.
sentences.zh: 如果没有，灯具会准确显示位置。

sentences.en: Either way, the measure is public and the same for everyone.
sentences.zh: 无论哪种方式，该措施都是公开的，并且对每个人来说都是相同的。

blocks.html_en: SHACKLE now ships first-class governance for the biggest chokepoints in the agent stack. See INTEGRATIONS.md for full usage and LiteLLM proxy config.yaml examples.

blocks.html_en:
• LiteLLM guardrail (shackle/litellm_shackle_guardrail.py) — enforce SP/1.0 on any LiteLLM-powered agent (CrewAI, AutoGen, LangGraph, custom):
    • ShackleGuardrail — Option A, backed directly by the pure SP/1.0 reference decide() (conformance-exact, dependency-light).
    • ShackleEngineGuardrail — Option B, driven by the full TriggerEngine (stateful budget / repeat / timeout).
    • Both provide sync check()/record() for the SDK and async_pre_call_hook/async_post_call_success_hook for the LiteLLM proxy. litellm is an optional dependency.

blocks.html_en: ⚡ The Problem

sentences.en: ⚡ The Problem
sentences.zh: ⚡ 问题

blocks.html_en: AI agents are highly capable, but their error-handling is fundamentally broken. When an agent hits an unhandled tool error (401 Unauthorized, changed API payload, dead endpoint), it rarely self-corrects. Instead, it enters a "Loop of Death" — retrying the exact same tool with the exact same input, burning your context window and running up massive API bills in minutes.

sentences.en: AI agents are highly capable, but their error-handling is fundamentally broken.
sentences.zh: 人工智能代理能力很强，但它们的错误处理能力从根本上被破坏了。

sentences.en: When an agent hits an unhandled tool error (401 Unauthorized, changed API payload, dead endpoint), it rarely self-corrects.
sentences.zh: 当代理遇到未处理的工具错误（401 未经授权、API 有效负载更改、死端点）时，它很少会自我纠正。

sentences.en: Instead, it enters a "Loop of Death" — retrying the exact same tool with the exact same input, burning your context window and running up massive API bills in minutes.
sentences.zh: 相反，它进入了“死亡循环”——使用完全相同的输入重试完全相同的工具，烧毁你的上下文窗口并在几分钟内运行大量 API 账单。

blocks.html_en: Frameworks like CrewAI, AutoGen, and LangGraph lack native, framework-agnostic spending guardrails or deterministic loop breakers.

sentences.en: Frameworks like CrewAI, AutoGen, and LangGraph lack native, framework-agnostic spending guardrails or deterministic loop breakers.
sentences.zh: CrewAI、AutoGen 和 LangGraph 等框架缺乏原生的、与框架无关的支出护栏或确定性循环断路器。

blocks.html_en: 🛡️ The Solution

sentences.en: 🛡️ The Solution
sentences.zh: 🛡️解决方案

blocks.html_en: SHACKLE is a lightweight, zero-dependency governance layer that sits inside your runtime via dynamic Python shims. It intercepts LLM calls and tool executions client-side, monitoring execution state deterministically.

sentences.en: SHACKLE is a lightweight, zero-dependency governance layer that sits inside your runtime via dynamic Python shims.
sentences.zh: SHACKLE 是一个轻量级、零依赖的治理层，通过动态 Python 垫片位于运行时内部。

sentences.en: It intercepts LLM calls and tool executions client-side, monitoring execution state deterministically.
sentences.zh: 它拦截 LLM 调用和客户端工具执行，确定性地监控执行状态。

blocks.html_en: When an agent breaches your boundaries, SHACKLE trips the circuit breaker, halts execution, and drops you into an interactive terminal console.

sentences.en: When an agent breaches your boundaries, SHACKLE trips the circuit breaker, halts execution, and drops you into an interactive terminal console.
sentences.zh: 当特工突破您的界限时，SHACKLE 会触发断路器，停止执行，并将您带入交互式终端控制台。

content_html_zh:
用于自主 AI 代理的运行时治理层以及 SP/1.0 一致性标准。 SHACKLE 实时调解每个代理工具调用：失控的令牌循环、未处理的工具级联和预算超支都会在下一次调用触发之前停止。 它已经上线，今天正在运行，并且其参考实现已证明通过了自己的一致性套件。 这不是提案或原型，而是您可以运行、验证和获得认证的工作标准。 （成本是在每次调用后从使用情况中读取的，因此 SHACKLE 会停止循环，而不是越界的单个调用；请参阅下面的 AGPL 尽力免责声明。）

🔒 SP/1.0 — 一致性标准

SHACKLE 不仅是一个运行时断路器，它还是为代理工具调用的运行时中介编写的、可验证的一致性标准。

  • 决策面：允许/拒绝/HITL
  • 一致性模型：有效(τ) ⇔ 必需(τ) ⊆ 支持(τ)
  • Fixtures/conformance.json 中的 15 个可哈希验证的一致性向量 — 10 个决策核心 + 5 个 HITL 转换案例（批准/拒绝/修改/推迟升级/重复恢复）
  • 纯参考实现：shackle/conformance.py — 仅 stdlib 的decide(config, state, call) -> (verdict, Reason)
  • 可执行证明：pytest测试/test_conformance.py根据参考运行每个向量
  • 核心不变量：历史可见≠运行时可执行 - 发生操作的记录并不能证明支持转换

运行时是 SHACKLE 一致的，当且仅当它通过了已发布的夹具集——可以通过复制而不是断言来证明。 请参阅 CONFORMANCE.md 了解完整规范以及如何声明一致性。 夹具哈希值已由第三方独立复制。

哪一层是哪一层：shackle/conformance.py + Fixtures/conformance.json 是一致性验证层——编写的规范及其 15 个可哈希验证的向量，并带有参考decide()。 shackle/core.py 是附带的运行时集成（@Guard 装饰器）：它将其实时 TriggerEngine/ExecutionState 映射到decide() 的（配置、状态、调用）合约，并在每个评估工具调用和每个LLM 调用上查阅相同的参考decide()，记录state.last_decision 上的判决。 因此，“符合 SP/1.0”是指规范、固定装置和交付运行时实际调用的参考实现 - 一个决策面，而不是碰巧一致的两个实现。

作者身份和出处：SHACKLE、所需⊆支持的一致性模型、decide() 表面和 HITL 转换合约由唯一作者 Dante Bullock (@Fame510) 撰写。 首次发布于 2026 年 6 月 17 日。

📜 SP/1.0 到底是什么

SP/1.0（SHACKLE 协议 1.0）是自主 AI 代理操作运行时调解的一致性标准。 简而言之：它精确地并且以任何人都可以独立验证的方式定义了系统正确决定是否允许代理采取下一步行动的含义。

每个人工智能代理的每一步都要做一些事情：调用工具、花费预算、调用另一个代理、执行交易。 SP/1.0 支配着那个时刻。 它指定了其他代理框架没有规定为可验证合约的三件事：

  1. 决策面 — 允许/拒绝/HITL。 每一项调解行动都会做出一个判决：允许、拒绝或停止。 没有第四种状态，也没有歧义。 每个判决都有一个确定性的、可检验的理由。
  2. 一致性模型 — 有效 (τ) ⇔ 必需 (τ) ⊆ 支持 (τ)。 当且仅当转换所需的一切都在系统可证明支持的范围内时，转换才是有效的。 这是该标准的数学支柱：能力是一组关系，而不是承诺。
  3. 核心不变量——历史可见≠运行时可执行。 记录、恢复或重放某个操作的事实并不能证明该操作曾被授权。 被拒绝或推迟的行动被拒绝，而不是被拒绝。 这条规则结束了代理“恢复”越过自己护栏的故障类别。

SP/1.0 提供 15 个可哈希验证的一致性向量 (fixtures/conformance.json) — 10 个决策核心案例加上 5 个人机循环转换案例（批准/拒绝/修改/延迟升级/重复恢复） — 以及仅 stdlib 的参考实现 (shackle/conformance.py)。 你没有凭信心接受这个标准。 你运行它。 pytest测试/test_conformance.py针对参考执行每个向量，并且参考实现通过其自己的套件 - 经过验证，而不是断言。

🎓 获得 SHACKLE 认证意味着什么

SHACKLE 认证是运行时正确执行 SP/1.0 中介契约的证明（而不是承诺）。 它完全是根据公共的、可哈希验证的装置来衡量的。 证明您的相同向量是其他任何人都可以重新运行以检查您的声明的向量。 没有私人审计，没有信任美国印章，没有付费通过：认证就是复制。

对于买家来说，“SHACKLE 认证”回答了每个认真的收购方、企业和监管机构最终都会询问的有关自主系统的问题：“你能证明它只做了允许做的事情，而不做其他事情吗？” 经过认证的运行时可以。 几分钟后。 来自干净的克隆。

认证级别

Level Name 它保证什么
SP/1.0-核心 核心一致性 运行时将每个中介操作解析为正确的 ALLOW / DENY / HITL 判决以及正确的拒绝原因。 决策面是健全的。
SP/1.0-HITL 过渡完成 核心，加上正确处理每个人在循环转换：批准、拒绝、修改、推迟升级和重复恢复。 事实证明，被拒绝和推迟的操作无法通过重播来执行。
SP/1.0-主权 企业运行时 HITL，加上原子守护程序状态、防篡改账本和审计导出。 企业或收购方可以追究责任的完整责任层。

如何获得认证（今天向任何运行时开放）

  1. 针对您的运行时运行该套件：pytesttests/test_conformance.py 和 pytest v2/tests/test_v2_conformance.py。
  2. 抓住证据。 SHACKLE 会发出每个夹具的通过/失败报告，其中包含其验证的夹具哈希值。 该报告是您的一致性工件——任何人都可以独立复制。
  3. 提交上市。 使用您的报告和公开的、可复制的证据链接打开认证请求。 自动检查重新运行公共设施；维护者在列出之前进行验证。
  4. 上市。 经过验证的运行时会出现在公共 SHACKLE 一致性注册表中，并包含所达到的级别、SP/1.0 版本和日期，并且可能会显示 SP/1.0 认证徽章。

开放的标准，敞开的大门

SP/1.0 是一个中立的公共标准。 邀请任何代理运行时（包括竞争框架和竞争安全产品）对其进行测试并列出。 赛程是公开的。 判决是确定性的。 注册表已打开。 我们将自己的参考实现与我们向其他人提出的要求完全一致，并且我们发布了我们的固定装置，以便可以检查声明。

这就是整个哲学：标准只有它能够承受的范围才有价值。 如果运行时符合要求，注册表就会证明这一点。 如果没有，灯具会准确显示位置。 无论哪种方式，该措施都是公开的，并且对每个人来说都是相同的。

认证您的代理商。 → 运行装置·获取列表·查看注册表

集成（LiteLLM + AutoGen）

SHACKLE 现在为代理堆栈中最大的瓶颈提供一流的治理。 请参阅 INTEGRATIONS.md 了解完整用法和 LiteLLM 代理 config.yaml 示例。

  • LiteLLM 护栏 (shackle/litellm_shackle_guardrail.py) — 在任何 LiteLLM 支持的代理（CrewAI、AutoGen、LangGraph、自定义）上强制执行 SP/1.0：ShackleGuardrail — 选项 A，直接由纯 SP/1.0 参考decide() 支持（一致性精确，依赖性轻）。 ShackleEngineGuardrail — 选项 B，由完整的 TriggerEngine（状态预算/重复/超时）驱动。 两者都为 SDK 提供同步 check()/record() ，为 LiteLLM 代理提供 async_pre_call_hook/async_post_call_success_hook 。 litellm 是一个可选依赖项。
  • AutoGen 包装器 (shackle/autogen_shackle_wrapper.py) —wrapp_tool 装饰器 + create_shackle_agent 工厂，通过同一引擎管理 AutoGen 工具调用。 AutoGen 是可选的；没有它，wrap_tool 也可以工作。

一次集成 = 覆盖整个供应链：由于 LiteLLM 位于大多数代理框架下，因此护栏通过单个阻塞点来管理它们。 所有决策都追踪到fixtures/conformance.json 中哈希固定的固定装置。

出处

SHACKLE 由来自加利福尼亚州奥克兰的 52 岁自学成才的系统架构师和工程师 Dante Bullock 建造。 没有风险投资。 没有企业孵化器。 只是原始的需要和拒绝看着自主代理在无声的无限循环中烧钱。

Sovereign Logic 没有猜测代理生态系统需要什么，而是使用实时网络抓取和社区情绪挖掘来审核 CrewAI、AutoGen 和 LangGraph 的问题跟踪器，绘制出影响生产中开发人员的确切系统故障，然后构建插入式断路器来修复它们。

这是由开发人员为开发人员构建的基础设施——主权、精益和零膨胀。

🎯 何时使用 SHACKLE

SHACKLE 专为以下用途而设计：

  • 本地开发和调试——交互式HITL控制台让您实时控制
  • CLI 代理和受监督的工作流程 - 检测到循环时恢复/跳过/中止
  • 跨框架覆盖——一个装饰器可以跨 CrewAI、LangGraph 和 AutoGen 使用
  • 预算执行——客户端代币跟踪可防止成本失控
  • 迭代测试——开发周期早期的 Catch 循环

对于无头生产 API（无服务器函数、FastAPI 端点、无法阻止人工输入的后台工作人员），请考虑用于自动 LangGraph 覆盖的 TokenCircuit 等框架本机解决方案。

SHACKLE 和面向生产的工具解决了互补的问题：在开发和测试期间使用 SHACKLE，然后根据需要过渡到已部署 API 的自动覆盖。

⚡ 问题

人工智能代理能力很强，但它们的错误处理能力从根本上被破坏了。 当代理遇到未处理的工具错误（401 未经授权、API 有效负载更改、死端点）时，它很少会自我纠正。 相反，它进入了“死亡循环”——使用完全相同的输入重试完全相同的工具，烧毁你的上下文窗口并在几分钟内运行大量 API 账单。

CrewAI、AutoGen 和 LangGraph 等框架缺乏原生的、与框架无关的支出护栏或确定性循环断路器。

🛡️解决方案

SHACKLE 是一个轻量级、零依赖的治理层，通过动态 Python 垫片位于运行时内部。 它拦截 LLM 调用和客户端工具执行，确定性地监控执行状态。

当特工突破您的界限时，SHACKLE 会触发断路器，停止执行，并将您带入交互式终端控制台。

主要特点

  • 1 行安装 — 无需重构您的代理拓扑
  • 死亡循环预防 — 检测相同的顺序工具调用和错误级联
  • 预算执行——针对客户端定价表进行实时代币跟踪
  • 执行超时——防止死 API 上的挂起线程
  • HITL 控制台 — 具有恢复/跳过/中止选项的交互式终端
  • 100% 客户端 — 无遥测、无回拨、无隐藏 SaaS

🚀 快速入门

1. 安装

注意：Shackle 在 PyPI 上作为 pyshackle 包发布（导入名称保持 shackle）。
# From source (available now)
git clone https://github.com/Fame510/SHACKLE.git
cd SHACKLE
pip install -e .

# From PyPI:
pip install pyshackle

2. 保护您的工作流程

from shackle import Guard
from crewai import Crew, Agent, Task

# Your normal CrewAI setup
my_crew = Crew(agents=[...], tasks=[...])

# One line to add circuit breaking
@Guard(budget=0.25, max_repeat_calls=3, timeout_seconds=180)
def run():
    return my_crew.kickoff()

run()

就是这样。 SHACKLE 动态挂钩底层解释器 — 无需更改 CrewAI 源代码。

⚙️ 四个断路器

扳机 健康）状况 默认 会发生什么
REPETITIVE_TOOL_CALL 同一工具 + 同一输入调用 N 次，或输入包含错误信号 3 次尝试 掉落至 HITL 控制台
BUDGET_EXCEEDED 累计代币成本超出限制（通过本地定价表） $0.20 硬执行冻结
超时_REACHED 挂钟执行超出阈值 180秒 立即停止
最多工具调用数 工具调用总数超出限制 50 calls 硬停止

误差环路放大

当工具输入包含错误信号（401、500、超时、未经授权等）时，SHACKLE 会增强灵敏度 - 在代理因无法修复的权限错误而烧毁令牌之前捕获“我会再试一次”循环。

🛠️ HITL 控制台

当断路器跳闸时，SHACKLE 会呈现一个交互式终端：

Tool:          web_search
Input:         {"query": "latest AI news", "error": "401 Unauthorized"}

🚀 V2：企业运行时主权层（可选）

对于需要分布式状态、合规性审核日志或远程代理控制的生产部署，请参阅 v2/README.md。

V2 adds:

🤝 贡献

定价表更新

当模型提供商更新定价时，将 PR 提交到 shackle/core.py → MODEL_PRICING。 提交经过验证的定价更新的贡献者将在发行说明中获得认可。

添加框架挂钩

SHACKLE 的架构支持可插入的运行时挂钩。 添加对新框架的支持：

  1. 按照 core.py 中的模式添加 _patch_<framework>() 函数
  2. 在 _apply_patches() 中注册它
  3. 提交带有集成测试的 PR
